description: Adds or updates a metrics panel that binds to a configured workbench metrics or time-series tool (CanvasBlock with type metrics and ToolGraph content). The UI will run the named tool with the given arguments to render charts or tables. Use `tool_name` that exists on this workbench and shape `tool_args` to that tool's input schema (PromQL, LogQL-style metrics, Datadog query, etc.). `layout` is required for grid position and size.

identifier.description: Unique key for this canvas block (e.g. `cpu_saturation`, `p99_latency`).

layout.description: Required grid placement (CanvasBlock.Layout). All of x, y, w, and h must be set.

x.description: Column index of the top-left cell (0-based).

y.description: Row index of the top-left cell (0-based).

props.description: Metrics widget configuration (WorkbenchJobResult.ToolGraph): human-readable framing plus the tool invocation.

title.description: Panel title shown above the metrics view (e.g. `Replica CPU throttling`).

summary.description: Optional short description of the signal and any caveats (sampling, aggregation window, etc.).

query.description: Which workbench tool to call and with what arguments (WorkbenchJobResult.ToolQuery).

tool_name.description: Name of the workbench tool to execute (must match a metrics-capable tool on this workbench).  This is likely reported in prior observability subagent results.

tool_args.description: JSON string passed verbatim to that tool's input schema (e.g. `query`, `step`, `time_range`).

summary.description: Optional note stored with the query metadata.
